They turned what was supposed to be a simple benchmark test and target practice into a full-blown naval exercise, completely unannounced.
Whoever green-lit the decision also deliberately omitted a combat script, saying that the exercise's scope depends on actual combat performance.

In other words, we don't know a damn thing. Nothing about what enemies we're going to be up against, what equipment they're going to have, what "unexpected situations" we'll need to prepare for, nada.
Apparently, they're also installing equipment to simulate certain combat environments, and we can expect state-of-the-art weaponry to be in play.
Given the scale of the exercise, expect it to be unlike anything you've ever seen before.
Mm... I get the feeling that there are gonna be a lot of unknown variables and unpleasant surprises thrown in.
I wonder why they're in such a hurry to push out all this new equipment that hasn't even been listed on the registries yet. It must be a really big deal to them.
The cutting-edge Type-II hull-fusion rigging hasn't been perfected yet, but it's apparently able to draw out a lot more power than before even in an unfinished state.
Therefore, it makes sense to want to get a benchmark of our new power as soon as possible so they have some ballpark numbers they can reference.
Now that's music to my ears. It's been a while since I've gotten to flex my muscles.
Plus, I really want to see what I'm capable of after all that sweat, blood, tears... and new gear!
Agreed. This is a good opportunity to challenge ourselves and test our mettle.
Knowing your strength on paper is good. Knowing your strength in practice is even better.
Heh. I had a feeling you'd all be on board.
Oh right, Doctor. Did you say something about the Professor participating in this exercise as well?
Well, naturally. Since the whole timetable got pushed up, that also necessitates some staffing changes.
After all, can you name a single person more fit to be a commander than my assistant here?
I see! Thanks, Dr. Anzeel, that works out perfectly then!
Heheh. You can repay me by putting on a stunning performance, then. Who knows, maybe you'll get to participate in more projects in the future as well!
If you impress the evaluators, maybe you'll get to spend more time working with the Professor here as well~
Work hard to get more work, huh? Sounds like a bad deal, but I'm in.
Agreed. I've never had a reason to doubt the Professor's knowledge or experience.
Alright then. Since we're all on the same page, I guess we'll be calling you Commander from now on~
Haha, I like the sound of that! Maybe I'll have to jump on the bandwagon as well~
What do you say to that, my assistant?
Commander. That is the title most familiar to me, after all.
"Commander" it is. We'll go through the exercise in greater detail later. For now, we've got another hour to ourselves before it's time to head to our accommodations for tonight.
Where are we staying? The university?
If only. I can pull strings and bend rules during daylight hours, but accommodations are outside my jurisdiction.
Sadly, we'll be staying at a base near the exercise area.
Darn. Oh well, that doesn't sound too bad.
Just with one tiny caveat. All their bedrooms are double rooms.
Wait, how do you know that?
Hehehe... I expected something like this might happen, so I did a bit of research.
Oh boy. So that means...
We'll all share a room with someone else, I suppose?
Bingo. Which leads us to the inevitable question–
Who gets to share a room with the Commander?
I know people who'd kill for that chance! Maybe it'll be me... Or maybe it'll be you.
Wh-why are you looking at me like that?!
You don't wanna? 'Kay, we can exclude Hammann~
Hang on! I didn't say anything I didn't want to–
Then you've still got a chance!
*sigh*... Hornet, please. You're making things hard for the Commander.
This is important. We have to work it out one way or another.
But hey, you could be the roomie arbitrator if you have a sound proposal.
Well... If the Commander doesn't mind, I'd–
Seriously? I leave for five seconds, and you all start arguing about who'll sleep with who?
Sorry to burst your bubble, but the Commander and I are staying in separate staff rooms in another building. We have an exercise to plan, y'know.
Laaame!
Do you have a problem with that? Would you rather take my place and spend all night planning?
You KNOW I can't do that! Fiiine. We'll discuss room sharing among ourselves.
Thought so. You have one hour before it's time to go. Decide before then.
Ooh, look! Street food! I used to visit the stands here all the time, and it looks like they've only gotten better since then!
Come with me, Commander. You've GOT to try some of this stuff!
Anzeel drags me right back outside to the street vendors. By the looks of things, this night is only just getting started...

Stacked Odds.say: The next day, at the exercise site...
The skies are calm today over the exercise site. As I wait aboard the command vessel, Yorktown and the other shipgirls are finalizing their preparations.
Though everyone's morale is initially high on the surface, that quickly takes a nosedive as I read off the information sent over by Memphis.
The enemy forces consist of everything from old and new mass-produced ships, to combat drones and unmanned carrier-based aircraft.
Not only that, their armaments and formations were specifically formulated to combat shipgirls.
By the looks of things, our opponents are far better prepared for this than any of us could have predicted...
I don't say this very often, but what the hell is going on? Their equipment is all cutting-edge stuff.
They have frigates with anti-air weaponry capable of shooting down entire waves of aircraft, and corvettes providing formidable close-range defenses...
That's not even mentioning their long-range anti-surface missiles that can track moving targets, their high-end radars, or their numerous jamming devices.
This is not a force of comparable strength. It's more like...
They're trying to gauge how we'd do in case of an alien invasion?
I can handle being the underdog, but this is ridiculous! What do they expect from us?!
This is gonna be so one-sided... Those weapons are straight out of science fiction!
One-sided is an understatement. That huge modern fleet, against three carriers, a destroyer, and a heavy cruiser.
Plus the Commander's unarmed command vessel, if you want to be generous...
As if we weren't already at a huge disadvantage, they've even got Memphis in command. Talk about "lured in by the dark side"...
Jeez, she could've at least told us where her forces are deployed before she swore to secrecy.
Unless she never meant to make it easy for us, and was just pretending to be all friendly yesterday...
That would explain why she showed up so late – she was perfecting her evil master plan!
Dr. Anzeel was with her, so she must've had SOME clue of what Memphis was up to.
I think she purposely told us nothing because she has faith in us and the Commander.
Plus, Memphis hasn't gotten the Type II upgrade, so she might not be able to join our side even if she wanted to.
At the end of the day, the purpose of this exercise is to test OUR abilities.
Regardless of which side achieves victory, we'll demonstrate our full capabilities.
It makes sense to over-tune the enemy as well, considering what we do know about the trajectory of the Revolutionary Front's E-Cube research.
Everyone, just have faith in yourself, and we'll be just fine.
Confidence ain't the problem here. Fairness is.
"Hornet has a point. The odds are heavily stacked against us in every meaningful metric."
"However, that does not mean that we don't have options."
"Let's think about frigates, first of all. They form a sturdy anti-air screen, but these mass-produced vessels must be controlled finely to achieve maximum effectiveness."
"Memphis can't possibly control them all by herself, meaning there have to be command ships that relay commands as intermediaries."
"Neutralize those, and the mass-produced ships will be thrown into disarray. They'll be sitting ducks."

"This will create an opening for us to focus an assault on the enemy line and take out their chain of command. Speedy elimination of key targets is one of the major advantages shipgirls have over conventional warships."
Basically, you're saying we may be outgunned, but have a huge tactical advantage?
"Yes. Your individual power trumps that of any mass-produced ship. If we're able to concentrate that power, we can turn the battle in our favor."
"Issuing the orders that lead us to victory – that will be my job."
The Commander is right. As long as we stay calm and seize whatever advantages we can get, victory isn't out of the question.
Studying modern and past warfare isn't enough. We have to look ahead at how warfare might be conducted in the future as well. The Commander has much to teach us about that.
"Please. I'm just speaking from experience."
Experience that I've accumulated over countless battles against the Sirens, leading shipgirls just like them.
Morning. Have you all finished strategizing?
Oh, you bet. You'll see soon.
Don't expect us to hold anything back with the Commander leading us.
You'd be wise not to underestimate us.
Of course not. I know that the Commander is extremely capable.
As for me, I'm good to go. This is no simulation, and I'm not gonna mess around.
Huh? Did you and the Commander run some sort of simulated exercise in preparation for this?
What? No, I meant... Mental simulations. Planning for your enemy's actions, that stuff.
While this is my first field exercise, I've done hundreds and hundreds of simulated ones before.
Woah, is that what you're doing all day in your room? And here I thought you were...
I'm starting to get nervous now...
Don't worry. Just keep your wits about you, and don't let the enemy get into your head.
Are we done chatting yet?
Dr. Anzeel?! Where were you this morning?
What do you think? Over at HQ, looking for a first-row seat and popcorn.
Let's quickly go over the rules one last time.
The red team's flagship is Yorktown, the blue team's is Memphis. First team to sink or effectively neutralize the other's flagship wins.
Simple enough. Everyone got that?
Understood.
Great. Then, let the Type II hull-fusion rigging test naval exercise commence!

Fusion Armaments.say: In the midst of the exercise...
Meager as our forces are, I still split them up into three groups.
I assign Northampton to Hornet, have Hammann and Yorktown pair up, and keep Langley with me by the command vessel.
Tons of enemy aircraft, incoming! Looks like they're from the carrier strike team! Memphis is finally putting her cards on the table!
Langley, give me a sitrep, ASAP!
I'm working on it! Just two more seconds...
Sent! The carrier strike team has several AA-focused corvettes as escorts! Be careful!
I'm still working on designating their command ships. Hold off your assault until I'm finished!
Roger!
Commander, Hornet's group is hanging in there, for now.
Unfortunately, things aren't looking very good. The enemy's strength is simply dumbfounding.
In addition to aircraft and drones, they've also deployed a lot of submersibles and mini-submarines.
While we do have the advantage in one-on-one fights, their swarms of expendable weaponry are starting to overwhelm us.
Anzeel gave me a report the night before which outlined the Type II rigging's construction and the functions it improved upon.
Chief among them are a general combat effectiveness increase, jamming countermeasures, and built-in electronic warfare capabilities.
Special mentions should also be given to the enhanced communications and information processing systems.
These allow shipgirls on the field to directly share info with each other via data links, and draw much more potential out of a shipgirl's hull.
This technology is the result of a production process that consumes additional Wisdom Cubes...
And research that, according to Anzeel, I played a significant role in.
"We have the upper hand when it comes to information sharing. We have to use it to our advantage."
You mean the data link? I guess so... The increased data processing power is one of the major selling points of the new rigging type.
This makes it possible to analyze data instantaneously and relay the results to shipgirls on the field in almost real-time.
It allows us to act as one, based on the orders you issue us.
Your terminal should be relaying information received from our end as well, right? It's an astronomical improvement over the back-and-forth radioing of the past.
"So, aircraft can be much better coordinated when they all have access to the same information, right?"
Correct! That's one of the features you helped develop.

You can also hand over control of certain reconnaissance equipment, something I took advantage of at the beginning of the exercise to continue gathering data while letting the others focus on the fight.
"Does the same apply to other carrier-based aircraft? Isn't it possible for carriers to share each other's air wings?"
Um, let me see... Oh, you can! I can't believe I overlooked that.
I'm glad you're here, Commander. You have a better grasp of the situation than we do individually.
The truth is, however... I don't know any more about this new rigging than they do. Maybe even less. They say I helped develop it, but I have no recollection of that at all.
"Yorktown, can you hear me?"
Yes, Commander?
"How's the situation on your end? Have you been able to cover the anti-submarine warfare?"
We've managed to deal with the underwater threat for now...
But, the air situation is still not looking too good. While I'm fighting for some airspace control, Hammann has to worry about both anti-ship and anti-submarine combat.
Sorry, but I won't be able to help the other two groups right now.
"Don't worry. In fact, I'm here to lend you a hand."
"Your recon planes aren't much use in a dogfight. Let Langley take control of them so she can pinpoint the location of the enemy command ships."
Give control to Langley? Okay, let me just... There, they're hers now.
Look out, Yorktown! We've got more mini-subs incoming!
Shoot, what terrifying numbers... Memphis really is going all-out this time.
"Hang in there a bit longer. We'll have our chance to turn things around soon enough."
Understood! I'll fight with all I've got!
"Langley, use all the recon aircraft at your disposal to mark those command ships."
"As soon as you do that, I'll give the order to have everyone focus fire on them."
"Then, while chaos spreads among the mass-produced ships, we'll deal a devastating blow to Memphis' main fleet and seize victory."
Roger!

Seizing the Advantage.say: KABOOOM!
All designated command ships have been successfully eliminated! The enemy line is in shambles!
I'll be damned... You actually did it, Commander!
Now that the enemy's been crippled, we'll ride the momentum and push forward as one, right?
"That's the plan. We'll still need to take out enemies sequentially, according to their relative levels of risk. I'll re-assess the situation from that point."
Yorktown! How did I do?
You were outstanding, Hammann. You managed to hold your own against enemy ships and subs, and bought me enough time to mount our counterattack.
Heehee~ Thank you!!
Hehe, piece of cake. Is that all those souped-up tin cans have to show?
Far from it. The battle isn't over yet.
I know, I know. I'm not as naive as I used to be.
As far as I can tell from the data links, Memphis' main fleet has been completely destroyed.
And yet, she's not said a word to us over the radio.
That's a bad omen. She's got to be planning a comeback.
If I was able to figure out her fleet's weakness, she's surely figured out mine as well.
That being, our carriers have an immense presence, but their protection is critically lacking.
I have successfully divided up her forces. However...
If she regroups outside our recon range, then launches a focused assault on us, we'll be in serious trouble.
Status update. The blue team's main fleet has been destroyed. While our shipgirls have sustained no damage, we have lost 40% of our aircraft.
Memphis has yet to make a clear move. What shall we do?
"For now, regroup in anticipation of an enemy ambush. Afterwards, we'll reorganize our groups."
"Hammann, Langley, you'll swap places and turn on your electronic warfare gear."
"Langley, I need you to send out a duplicate of Hammann's signal near Yorktown, while Hammann turns her signal off altogether."
I've read all the materials about the Type II that I could get my hands on, and I'm still not sure if my proposal is actually doable...
Understood. I can do that.
What are you planning, Commander? This sounds like it'll make you look unprotected...
Wait... Don't tell me you're going to use yourself as bait!
"Correct. It will lure Memphis into attacking me while giving your group more fighting power."
"It's a risky plan, but it's worth a shot."
As you say... I'll scramble my planes as soon as anything happens. Just be careful!
Looking at the radar, it seems we have a bite already. She's sending her reserve fleet this way.
Her RESERVE fleet? She really was waiting for an opportunity like this!
"Our aircraft will make short work of them before they get here, and she must know that. This reeks of a distraction to me..."

Hard-Fought Victory.say: Later in the exercise...
The girls are doing a good job holding back the reserve fleet, but there are so many enemies to deal with that they can't focus on anything else.
Everyone is fighting hard, except for Hammann, who's with me on the command ship's deck.
She's pacing around anxiously, keeping an eye on my device for updates on the battle.
Why am I sitting around while everyone else is fighting for dear life?
The Commander clearly made the wrong call here...
"What's wrong, Hammann? Do you have a problem with my deployments?"
Huh? I guess. I just wish I was doing something to help. I'm not mad at you or anything...
They're handling themselves well, but that doesn't change the fact...
...Wait! A blip just appeared on our sonar! An enemy is nearby!
A big one, too. It must be a big submarine... No, it's the size of a battleship!
Heads up! It's resurfacing!
The seas rumble, and a massive, silver-clad vessel emerges from the depths.
Just as I anticipated, Memphis' reserve fleet is only a diversion. This mega-submarine is the real threat to us.
"Interesting exterior. It looks a lot like the submarine Belorussiya's crew used when I went to the Northern Parliament."
This is no Northern Parliament vessel. She's the Epimetheus, a Federation mega-submarine for deep-sea exploration.
She's outfitted with a range of sensors and supercomputers that processes data from the ocean floor just as well as the battlefield.
And not only that, her cannons pack more than enough firepower to blow your command ship to kingdom come!
Heheh... Sounds like you're lost for words.
They let you have a submarine that can dive out of our sonar range?!
How is that fair?! That's literally cheating!
Cry about it. Even if you scramble your planes, it's too late.
I'm so sorry, Commander. I had no clue she'd made it right next to us...
"It's okay. She should have no idea YOU'RE here either. Strike now while she's unaware!"
Got it! I'll show her what happens when you mess with us!
KABOOOM!
Memphis' mega-submarine is a very different beast to an ordinary mass-produced ship, but still, it's not designed specifically for combat.
A shipgirl should easily prove a fair fight for it.
"You're not the only one with a trump card. You might want to–"
Back down? I don't think so!
Didn't you hear me? This baby has supercomputers and advanced sensors onboard!
I knew Hammann was with you from the moment I reached the surface!
That's why I resurfaced from a distance – to lure her away from you while I do... THIS!
Target: deck of the command ship at twelve-o'-clock!
Activate escape pod! Bail out!
KABOOOM!
An oval-shaped object shoots out of her mega-submarine, coming straight towards me.
It lands just nearby on the deck, then burst open to reveal Memphis, rigging at the ready.
Checkmate, Commander.
While you did display excellent knowledge of tactics and mastery of the Type II rigging's functions, you did not anticipate me boarding you.
You did well, but I was one step ahead.
Sure, I didn't anticipate a tactical use of her escape pod, but I DID plan for something similar.
Thank you. I contemplated a thousand different ways to beat you, and eventually arrived at this one.
"I see. You deserve a promotion. The battle isn't over yet, though."
It will be once I neutralize you. Without someone to command your team, it will... Huh?
Wait... What are those planes?
"My contingency plan in case you were to board my command vessel through some means."
"Open fire!"
Grab on to something, Commander!
KABOOOM!
Blue team's flagship has been destroyed. The exercise is over. Red team has won.
Wow... I applaud you, Commander. I thought I planned for everything, and still you were one step ahead.
Holy smokes, what a finale! I was on the edge of my seat there.
You really deserve a round of applause, Commander. I get why everyone adores you now.
Both sides, great job. This was truly a spectacular test. Expect a juicy reward for all your efforts later.
It better be good! We worked our asses off today, and with minimal time to prepare!
Yeah... Sorry 'bout that. On the bright side, we have the next few days off while the team analyzes the exercise data.
Heck, we can take my plane and go on a short vacation somewhere! The Commander would probably like that.
An actual vacation? Really?
Aww hell yeah! Commander, Dr. Anzeel, you're the best!
Hang on, someone's calling... Oh, it's Aoste. Just a minute.
...Anzeel speaking. What's up?
...I mean, what do you think? The test went perfectly.
...Yeah, I guess it was a given, with the Commander at the helm.
...You did that? That totally explains why the blue team was overpowered in quantity AND quality.
...Come again?
...You know me too well. Data will take a few days to process, so yeah, might as well take some time off.
...You're serious? Okay. I dig the sound of that.
...Yeah, I'll try to explain it to the Commander. Talk to you later.
What was that about, Dr. Anzeel?
Nothing important. We just talked about vacation destinations.
He told me about a pretty sweet place, actually. Ever heard of Samos Island?

Linked Lens.say: Sea of Stars Research Base - Core Area, Sector X
Overflow resolved. Reality Lens data analysis program functioning normally.
Maintaining crisis response. Temporal regulator restored to 95% functionality.
Subjective time ratio has been restored to 67%.
Thank goodness... We were seconds from disaster.
We've regained control of the temporal regulator, so I think we're well on our way to saving the Commander.
Helena, can you release the safety lock?
Understood. Releasing emergency safety lock... And done!
TB, disconnect the Commander and restore consciousness.
Orders received. Proceeding with emergency rescue sequence—
Warning: Unable to restore connection with the Commander's consciousness.
Warning: Unable to confirm Commander's presence in Virtual Space 3.
Warning: Unable to monitor virtual space.
Conclusion: The Commander does not currently exist in the virtual space constructed from Anchorage's hull data.
Does not currently exist...? Umm, are you saying the Commander is not in her dream?
...In other words, the Commander is no longer inside the Reality Lens?!
TB, please! Tell us what's happened!
Apologies, I am unable to disclose that information through my language programs.
The Commander's body exists within the Reality Lens. The connection between the Commander's consciousness and the Reality Lens is stable.
However, I am unable to locate the Commander's consciousness, and therefore cannot monitor the virtual space.
Hypothesis: The Commander's consciousness has been transferred to an inaccessible space.
An inaccessible virtual space? TB, aren't you the administrator of the Reality Lens?
I've never heard of any areas in your own mainframe that you don't have the power to monitor.
Per protocol, I am not authorized to disclose this information. Insufficient clearance.
Everyone, just calm down! TB, I need you to tell me one thing. Is the Commander's consciousness safe?
Query received. Currently attempting to trace the Commander's consciousness–
The Commander is still connected to the Reality Lens. Therefore, it must still exist.
That doesn't tell us much... Okay. Let's try this.
The Commander's body and mind are still here in the Reality Lens.
If we give the order to shut down the device, the Reality Lens should automatically disconnect from the Commander as a safety precaution.
It might result in some confusion once this is over, but if nothing else, it should save the Commander.
Think it'll work, TB?
In theory, it should be possible.
Alright, let's try it. Shut down the Reality Lens and bring the Commander's mind back.
Orders received. Shutting down the Reality Lens.
Shutdown program halted. Administrative permissions required.
Wait, what's going on? I thought you had the highest level of administrative privileges!
The authorizations required to use the shutdown program have changed. I do not have access to this function.
I will need to be granted permission or a temporary authorization code to complete this action.
The authorizations... have changed? We didn't do anything of the sort...
Did someone secretly modify the authorization requirements after the experiments started? But how...?
Memphis! Now isn't the time to worry about that.
We need to focus on what we can do for the time being!
Y-yeah. You're right... Helena, can you continue monitoring the situation?
Will you... help us again...?
Helena? What's going on?
Perhaps you, of all people... could successfully hack into the Sea of Stars.
Can I... trust you?
Can I trust you... "Helena?"
Helena, who are you talking to?
Emergency control code: 47741 30130 30146. Authorizing agent: "Helena."
I know how we can save the Commander.
Huh...?
TB, transfer control of the Tower to "Helena."
Orders received. Transferring control now.
Emergency access code confirmed. Permissions transfer complete.
Please input coordinates from the Core Area.
Orders received. Please input next set of coordinates.
Now, please grant me permission to track the virtual space.
Understood. It's up to you now, Helena...
Helena murmured, seemingly to herself, and typed a code into the control apparatus.
Orders received. Tracking virtual space.
Helena, what did you just do?
I didn't know TB... heck, the entire Sea of Stars had this function.
How did you do all that? And who were you talking to just now...?
S-sorry, it's a bit complicated...
If you bear with me, I'll try to explain after we rescue the Commander...

WFH 'n' Chill Mk. 1.say: [Reality Lens Replicated Space] [Location: Chartered plane WFH 'n' Chill Mk. 1] [Cognitive merging data unknown]
As soon as we get our luggage sorted, Anzeel plops down on a plush couch inside her jet's cabin and gazes out the window.
Ooh, that's nice... I tell ya, there's no place like home.
I did a full day's work. Finally, time to kick back and relax...
"Speaking of home, I've been wondering about this plane's name."
Hm? You got a problem with the name I gave it?
"...Never mind, actually."
What's with that look on your face? C'mon, grab a coffee and take it easy.
I accept the cup of hot coffee Anzeel hands me. She goes right back to flipping through some documents.
Not exactly my idea of "taking it easy."
Or maybe, reading something is just a way for her to clear her head.
I've probably been overthinking this too much. I'm not going to get more information about this world if I don't ask her.
Last night was a bit too hectic to even make small talk, but this is a good opportunity.
What are you staring at me for?
Oh, I get it. You're part of the tea faction, aren't you?
Where should I even begin...?
The one where the fleet travels through time?
Pretty crazy how Bonnie got the lead role in that movie, huh?
I already knew though, since I got to sit in on their internal screenings.
Y'know, I would've at least liked to be one of the extras...
But it just didn't mesh with my schedule, so I couldn't.
Oh, right. You wanted to ask me about something, huh? Maybe time travel?
I'm the kind of person who'd try to change the past, no doubt about it.
Sure, it might cause the preordained future to unravel, but that's not a bad thing, is it?
If anything, doesn't that mean there's a chance to create a better future?
In the end, isn't that what humans are always trying to do, with or without time travel?
For the sake of creating a better future, I think changing the past is perfectly reasonable. Maybe even morally correct.
But, Aoste probably wouldn't agree with me on that one.
"Speaking of Aoste, what has he been up to recently?"
That guy... What doesn't he research? His team has gotten so big these days that he has his hands in every cookie jar at once.
After all, he's now our prized Renaissance man, master-of-all-trades.
I think he's been focusing on AI research these days though.
I think you've already met his favorite. Does "Zero" ring a bell?
She's part of Project ANTI-X. They seem to be next-generation humanoid weapons.
...Zero?
Suddenly, Yorktown comes up to us, holding a tray of pastries.
Oh, Yorktown. Need something?
Doctor, Commander. Would you like some sweets?
The new instant mechanical confectioner is working very well.
Sweets, huh? Wait, come again? An instant mechanical confectioner?
I said I'd pay for souvenirs, not whatever that is!
Spill the beans, Yorktown! Who did the deed?! You? Hornet? Or maybe the Commander?
Hornet bought it. Erm, she said it seemed like fun to make sweets in a chartered plane...
And she got you hooked, huh?
I get it. What's done is done, and if it tastes good, maybe it's not all bad...
Despite her grumbling, she gets up and makes her way to the mechanical confectioner.
"The facilities and furniture in this charter plane are too nice to be just a private plane, aren't they?"
The military provided this plane for the doctor.
As I recall, some corporate alliance from the Union used cutting-edge Energy Cube technology to make it. It can fly smoothly in any weather.
Heehee. This is my first time riding, too, Commander.
Commander, this mechanical confectioner is awesome!
It looks like Yorktown's going to tell me something, but Anzeel interrupts with her shouting from the hallway.
It's seriously so cool! You gotta try it out! I mean, it can make anything!
Shall we, Commander?

Samos Island.say: Anzeel's charter plane lands at the Samos Island airport and we disembark.
Before I go, let me leave you with a gift. You'll want to pay a visit to Samos Island.
All I can say is that you'll find something of intrigue there.
"Something of intrigue, huh. That's so vague I'm not sure what to think of it."
If I knew what it is, I would tell you a little more. Sadly, I don't.
Friedrich has mentioned Samos Island, too...
But will I really find that thing of intrigue here? In this virtual space?
Bright sunlight, sandy beach... Now that's a vacation island for you.
It's like an island resort!
Right? I told you we were gonna party.
You also said this place had a secret military research facility. I don't see anything like that, though...
Should they be letting tourists get so close to a military facility in the first place?
I forgot that this was your first time here. This is how some secret research facilities are.
It's not the facility that needs to be hidden; it's the research.
You never know. Maybe that university had a lab where they conduct secret experiments right under the café!
Anyway, it's not like this facility isn't hidden.
Outsiders think it's just the Samos Marine Research Institute, though.
A long time ago, they actually used to research marine life here.
One day the military took over, and it became Dr. Aoste's research facility.
I think he had his reasons for picking this place.
But hey, it's a tourist destination. Maybe he just picked it so he could hang out on the beach when he feels lazy.
Ooh! Commander, did everyone bring swimsuits?
Umm... I didn't.
Me, neither. I'm sorry, Commander...
That goes to show just how few of us seriously see this as a vacation.
You heard them, Commander. You'd better go get some swimsuits!
"We didn't come here just to have fun, did we?"
Forget about that for now! Right now, we gotta think of how to get these girls ready to swim!
Oh, but don't worry about buying swimsuits for everyone.
Aoste has facilities that can make gear for each individual guard on demand, so I bet there's something here that can make swimsuits and other clothes!
Maybe it even spits out certain designs on demand!
"Would he really have equipment like that?"
Should we be wasting time on the beach...?
Don't worry about it! You came all this way to help, so we gotta let you indulge yourselves a little!
As long as it's okay...
The facility's just through these woods. Make sure you keep up! We don't want anyone getting lost.

Zero?.say: After a walk through the woods, we reach an aging facility.
Authenticating access, aaand... Done.
Heheh. Open sesame!
Welcome, one and all, to Aoste's own research center: the Samos Marine Research Institute!
Th-thanks...
Geez, temper your excitement. This is THE secret research facility! Aren't you excited at all?
It's kinda plain. Like, it's just... a research facility, like you'd expect.
If you're gonna camouflage your secret research facility, then go all the way! That's basically Aoste's motto.
Is that... a room full of fish tanks?
Yep! That's a remnant of when this place actually performed marine research.
Wow, and it's so well-maintained. They've even got fish in there... Now I'm hungry.
Is fish the main course at the cafeteria here?!
Poultry is, actually. But they do serve fish.
This one time back at university, Aoste and I visited the aquarium together.
The exhibits were, well, okay. But their restaurant's specialty was grilled squid!
So you'd see squids in the exhibits, and then you'd have to eat their relatives...
Let me tell you, I remember crying while I put lemon on it more than I remember the aquarium itself!
Yorktown, you ought to take the Commander and Enterprise out on a date there sometime!
Eugh... They didn't cook the squids from the exhibits, did they...?
Of course they did! They had other stuff like that, too~
Now that I think about it, maybe Aoste chose this Marine Research Institute because of the trauma...
Anyway, the boring building up ahead is our destination.
You'd never know what kind of research is going on here unless you went in there!
Me and the others set foot inside the building's main hall. Then, something unexpected happens.
There's a panel next to the entrance. It glows and starts projecting a hologram.
The hologram looks familiar. Is that...?
Welcome back, Dr. Anzeel.
TB?!
Lookin' cute as ever, Zero. Is Aoste in?
Yes. He has been awaiting you.
Unfortunately, the doctor is currently preoccupied with work. You cannot meet him quite yet.
So for the time being, I will be your guide around the facility.
Subject for Artificial Navy Transformational Intelligence, Class X is ready for testing. Will you be observing?
Yes, please lead the way. Also, that full name is too much of a mouthful. Just go with Antiochus, alright?
Understood. I will now guide you to the testing area.
There's a slight beneath my feet. Most likely some sort of machine springing into action.
Then, the floor ahead of us splits in two, forming a set of staircases that lead down.
I presume this is our way to the secret institute.
Right you are.
Now come along and I'll show you what the "Marine Research Institute" REALLY works on!

Antiochus.say: [Reality Lens Replicated Space] [Location: Samos Island, Secret Underground Laboratory] [Cognitive merging data unknown]
Just how far underground are we?
Good question. I'd estimate around 20 or 30 meters.
With how many branching paths by now?
I'm not sure... Why do you ask?
Thought I'd explore this place, but I don't wanna get lost. How many do you reckon, Commander?
I do not endorse moving through the facility without a Navigator.
Each and every corridor here leads to a different testing area, all of which are isolated from each other.
Furthermore, you would not make it past the entrance to any of them without the proper clearance.
In the event of a breach, the security systems will engage and sound an alarm. Please be aware of that.
Oh, then I won't. Sorry.
It's alright. Simply follow my lights and there will be no issue.
It's so dark and spooky in here...
I don't like it... I wanna go back to where we were before...
You'll have to hang in there a bit longer, Hammann. Dr. Aoste has a very different personality than what we're used to.
The Commander's with us, so just bear with it, okay?
Hrmm... I figured something like this would happen.
Knew what would happen?
More often than not, having the backing of the military isn't a good thing.
I can tell, that guy's under a ton of pressure right now.
He not only has his own projects to attend to but also has to deal with the whims and never-ending demands of the military folks.
His project team has gone through several rounds of expansion, fragmenting, and reorganizing, all the while their workload continues to grow.
With deadlines to meet and confidentiality levels to balance, keeping this place running must be an absolute nightmare
Basically, there's a laundry list of reasons why the facility looks the way it does. Try not to take it out on the staff, alright?
Having that said, we're not some formal government delegation either. Just follow me and don't go running around, and we shouldn't have any problems.
I see... Everyone has their own share of troubles, huh?
I can manage that. Not like this is my first choice for a date spot either, hehe~
Thank you for your understanding in this matter.
We're almost there. Please stand behind the yellow line while the doors open.
I do so as instructed, and the massive doors shielding the research division part for us, revealing a lab that seems to double as a manufacturing facility.
The assembly workshop is open. Standing by pending further directives.
The experimental ANTI-X vessel scheduled to participate in the upcoming test is just ahead. Please wait for further instructions from Dr. Aoste.
The hologram of "Zero," the girl who looks uncannily like TB, disappears after finishing her words.
Whoooa! This place is huuuge!
Look at all the equipment hanging from the ceiling... I can't even imagine how much that must've cost.
Incredible... A single one of those glowing-blue facilities must be worth more than Dr. Anzeel's plane...
Look over there. That corridor seems to lead to some kind of... harbor?
Even though we're deep underground, they even dug out an underground waterway.
I wonder where it leads...
The immense facility before me has me lost for words.
Row after row of highly sophisticated machinery, occupying enough space to fit several football fields. Another passage that leads even deeper underground. But most interesting of all...
Is a girl with black hair sitting on the railing in the hall, her back against an enormous metallic contraption.
Woah, look at that, my assistant! Isn't that freakin' amazing?
The figure in front of me exudes a familiar, oppressive aura––
So much so that the instant I see her, a single word starts to wrack my mind: Arbiter.
I can't believe my eyes! Her hair... isn't white!!
Zero, come here! I need to know this girl's name!
Acknowledged. This is a vessel for "The Devil XV."
Another Major Arcana name, huh? Aoste, you edgy lad.
"The Devil XV? I knew it... That's just like the designation of the Sirens' Arbiter Program!"
Anzeel also just said she's Dr. Aoste's creation... Is he the father of all Arbiters – or even of all Sirens?
Project ANTI-X... Antiochus... So it wasn't a coincidence after all...
The attack on NY City... was no more than a ploy devised by the Antiochus. Its true purpose was for us to meet one another.
As for us, we are but Ember and Ash in the wind. You need not concern yourself with our names.
I remember the first time I heard that word, "Antiochus."
"Artificial Navy Transformational Intelligence, Class X." The Sirens' true name...
Wait, why is the Reality Lens showing me this? And why am I taking this information at face value?
Commander, are you okay? You look pale.
If none of this is actually real, why aren't I doing something to get back to reality?
I have to know. Someone needs to tell me what's real.
She let something slip about the "Reality Lens" earlier. She has to know something.
Huh? Commander, what are you saying...
Are you alright? And what are these "Sirens" you keep talking about?
Memphis's reaction to that word was clear and immediate. She's related to my current situation, but how exactly?
She doesn't seem to be acting maliciously, but why isn't she telling me anything?
Hey, I'm not gonna let you squirm your way out of an explanation after you said all that.
You said something about "Sirens" and some "Arbiter Program." Never heard of those things before, but they definitely sound like Aoste lingo.
*gasp*... Have the two of you been secretly working on a project behind my back?!
So... Those words just came to you in a dream?
I suppose that's not too unusual in this line of work.
See, while dreams are usually reconstructions of your memories, sometimes they'll hit you with something you've never even thought of.
Dreams follow their own rules. Sometimes they can't be logically explained, other times they can't be explained at all.
The brain has a tendency to mash things together and recombine them into things that you perceive as entirely new information. Happens to me all the time.
For all we know, these "Sirens" could be a mishmash of mythology, horror, and sci-fi.
(Psst, Hornet. Why is she giving the Commander a lecture on dreams?)
(Beats me. All I know is we have to step in, or she'll never stop.)
(Hey, Zero. Can you power up that Antiochus vessel?)
(Yes. The Devil is in standby mode and ready to be activated whenever desired.)
While Yorktown and Hornet whispered about something among themselves, Anzeel kept going and going.
(Then do me a favor. Wake Devil up and tell it to draw the doctor's attention.)
(Understood. Initiating program.)
Suddenly, the machine behind the black-haired Arbiter begins to whirr softly.
A few moments later, she stretches her arms, and listlessly opens her eyes.
Hmm...? Who woke me up?
Oh, must've been you guys. Guessing you're with Dr. Anzeel.
Welcome to the Samos Marine Research Institute. I'm The Devil XV. Just "Devil" is fine.
I've received the plans for our test and am ready to get started.
Lemme know if you need something.
...Holy shit.
She's so cute I'm gonna get diabetes!
I've never seen Anzeel run before, but she closed the gap between her and the Devil in a terrifyingly short span of time.
She whips her phone out, buzzing around the bored-looking girl, snapping pictures of her from every which way.
Hook, line, and sinker! The Commander's free!
Awwwww! You're the sweetest little devil ever!
Let's see a smile! Look into the camera!
Yeah, that's perfect!
Three, two, one!
Doctor, everything in this facility is absolutely top secret.
Any pictures you take will be deleted on the way out.
Dammit, I forgot!
Aww... Wait, I've got it!
I can send these pics to Aoste before they get deleted...
Then after we've left, I'll ask him to send them to me, citing communication between project members. I'm a genius!
That's not a solution, Doctor. That's leaking classified material.
No it's not! It's perfectly within the rules!
Commander, Dr. Anzeel. Dr. Aoste's work has concluded. He is waiting for you in his office.
Whoa! That's no hologram, that's actually her in the flesh!
Uooooh! She's even cuter in person!
Anyway, come along, Commander! It's time you met Aoste.
Everyone else, you can stay here and chat with Devil.

The Magister.say: We make our way to Dr. Aoste's office.
That will be all, Zero. Go work on something else.
As you command.
So, Commander, what do you think of the new Antiochus?
They put Anzeel's silly puppets to shame, wouldn't you agree?
All I have to say is that they're leagues cuter than the previous ones. I was getting tired of the white hair.
"I must admit, many of your Antiochus do share common features... Was that by design, Dr. Aoste?"
Yes. Reusing a tried and tested design is efficient. Unlike someone here, I don't concern myself with frivolous appearances.
Bite me. Anyway, let's get to the point.
You didn't call us all the way here just to show off your new Antiochus, did you?
Be honest with me here. You dabbled in some tech you don't understand, didn't you?
Indeed... The newest Antiochus model has something that the prior ones did not, something akin to your Wisdom Cubes.
Just as the two of you have been trying to incorporate Wisdom Cubes into the Type II rigging, I have been applying a similar technology to the latest generation of Antiochus vessels.
You... You made these Antiochus with Wisdom Cubes?!
How is that even possible? They don't even have hull data to pull from!
Hull data stores the embodiment of human emotions associated with the conceptual existence of a warship...
You can't just reach into a magic hat... How can you use Wisdom Cubes to give form to something from pure fiction?
You're correct. Allow me to explain – the Antiochus are fundamentally different from your KAN-SEN.
KAN-SEN draw information from the Wisdom Cube and are embodied as individuals with personalities based upon the concepts and emotions imprinted in their hull data.
On the other hand, the Antiochus are simply androids with an anthropomorphized personality installed. In that sense, my creations are closer to puppets than yours.

But that isn't important. What matters is that the Commander developed the Type II rigging using Wisdom Cubes not to create KAN-SEN, but to make a conceptual weapon capable of augmenting their hull data.
That inspired me to devise a similar process. Using Energy Cubes, I tapped into the concept of a weapon that does not rely on core data, and manifested that instead.
Just as the Commander managed to add information to hull data through the Type II rigging, I used a similar process to add information to the Antiochus using Energy Cubes.
Furthermore, these new weapons are modular in nature and not locked to any particular individual. One of your more worthwhile ideas to borrow, Anzeel.
Commander, as you already know, I am more interested in technology than playing with Anzeel's dolls. No offense to you, of course.
At a fundamental level, the Antiochus are built with a sophisticated AI program at their core. These vessels, just like your riggings, can be freely replaced.
All in all, I suppose you could say that I took advantage of a little loophole.
Wow... Have to hand it to you – well done, Aoste.
I presume Devil back there has gear made from E-Cubes. She must be a lot stronger than the previous generation.
That goes without saying. The last technical obstacle to their mass production will soon be solved, too, once I've finalized my theory.
I can't tell you how close I am, I'm afraid. You'll see for yourself later during our joint experiment.
Oh, forgive me, I never addressed your initial question, Anzeel.
The reason I asked you both to come is that I need your help on this upcoming experiment. There's a risk I want to eliminate at all costs, you see.
I thought you'd forgotten after you went on that admittedly enlightening tangent.
I will say... If you're actually able to get the Antiochus to the mass-production stage, they will without a doubt define the next era of warfare.
When that day comes, the true nature of their name will be realized.
Might go from eXperimental to Xenotermination if they'll be fighting aliens. Commander? You look like you have a question.
Here I am, in a room with the Creator of the shipgirls and the Magister of the Sirens... Am I not one step away from answering the question I've had for so long?
Assuming this world truly is woven from memories and not from fantasy, that is.
"Or a shipgirl is born with a defect in her hull data... Is it possible to cure those conditions using this technology?"
Wow, THAT came out of left field. I mean, theoretically, it should be possible, but would depend on the nature and quality of the damage.

The Type II fusion-hull process pulls information out from a secondary "mainframe," embodies that information in the form of a rigging, and, as the name implies, fuses it with the original shipgirl's hull data.
The presence of a secondary "backup" Wisdom Cube not only strengthens the existing hull, but also provides a way to bypass certain levels of damage to the primary Wisdom Cube.
Think of it as using a cloned liver to treat someone with renal disease.
Eww, gross! Although, that is a pretty apt comparison.
For example, take Yorktown, who was given the Essex-class Type II rigging. That managed to postpone the issues we were experiencing with the original Yorktown-class hull.
Unfortunately, this method is only a stopgap measure. It cures the symptoms, not the disease itself.
Just as in the case of humans, backups can fail. Under normal conditions, the sub-Cube will be able to compensate for damages caused to the main Cube.
But if this damage becomes too severe, we likely won't be able to use this trick twice.
Furthermore, it's best not to kick the can down the road too much. Once a Wisdom Cube has been irreparably damaged, even the Type II rigging won't be able to reverse its course.
To truly rid ourselves of these aberrations requires nothing short of a miracle – and an enormous amount of Wisdom Cube resonance.
"Wait... So with enough Wisdom Cube resonance, there might be a way?"
Which is a serious drawback compared to my Antiochus. They're only programs inhabiting artificial bodies.
Their bodies and weaponry are expendable and replaceable ad infinitum.
All they need is a functioning mainframe to take control of a new vessel.
And if their mainframe is destroyed, they only need a replacement to reboot the program.
Should an individual program suffer an error, it will be swiftly corrected as long as they're connected to another terminal.
If a program is not connected, its access to key equipment is restricted. Moreover, rogue actors can be swiftly identified and neutralized.
"Neutralized"... Sheesh, that's extreme.
"Neutralize" is not always a euphemism for "kill," Anzeel. It simply means taking measures to ensure the safety of it and other programs.
Losing connection to the network in and of itself is an error. This expedites search and recovery efforts for programs in danger.
Are you convinced by the Antiochus' potential yet, Commander? I'd love to have you on the project. I could even put you in charge of your very own team.
Hey! Stop trying to poach MY assistant!
Very well. Then, let's move on to the next order of business – the experiment.
Sure, but I want to have a few words with you. Can we leave the experiment to my assistant here?
That was my original plan. As I told you before, I won't interfere with the KAN-SEN in any way. Devil will manage things on the Antiochus' end.
Good. Commander, show us how it's done.
Soon, Anzeel and Aoste step through a door to the side of the office, heading deeper into the laboratory.
I am left by myself. Unsettling, alien thoughts start to creep into my mind.
It reminds me of what I experienced while talking to Anzeel back at the café.
A strange case of deja vu, as if I wasn't hearing this conversation for the first time.
Aoste did not seem to be responding to my question, but rather addressing his own agenda.
...But, I can't dwell on that right now.
The experiment is about to begin. They're all waiting for me.
I place my hand on the door at the back end of the office and push.
It doesn't open. It refuses to open. It doesn't seem like it can open, as if my mind recognizes it as a wall instead of a door.
Wait, where have I seen this room before? ...Recorder?
Yes, it was when I entered the Reality Lens. There was the AI that called itself "Recorder."
Commander? Dr. Anzeel told me she put you in charge of the experiment.
Everyone's ready to go. We're just waiting on you now.
I reach for the door again, but Memphis's voice snaps me to my senses.
...Something tells me that she's here to stop me.
Come on, I'll take you there!

The Devil XV.say: [Reality Lens Replicated Space] [Location: Sea near Samos Island] [Cognitive merging data unknown]
So, before we get this thing started, a quick self-introduction.
My model ID is The Devil XV, but you can just call me Devil.
I'm a second-generation Antiochus specializing in battlefield support. Frankly, taking head-on fights isn't my forte.
The purpose of today's experiment is to gauge how Antiochus and KAN-SEN fight both against and alongside each other. Mostly the latter.
In other words, we do some target practice first, then we shoot at each other?
Yep. Thanks for summarizing that for me.
Are there any other Antiochus coming? Surely you don't mean it's all of us against you alone...?
I've got mass-produced weapons on my side, but apart from them, I'm the only second-generation vessel.
Guess we'll have to hold back. We don't wanna damage Dr. Aoste's beloved vessel.
Oh, don't worry; nobody wants you to take it easy on me. This is a test of my combat abilities, so give me everything you've got.
I sent you data on the test plan as well as the specs of my vessel and weapons, so give it a peek if you want.
Ooh, let's see... Huh? Why isn't my communicator working?
It's, like, overloaded or something... Northampton, can I have some space?
Is it that much data? Okay, I've given you storage permissions.
Goodness... I'm not exaggerating when I say that this is an enormous amount of data.
I only glanced at the data headers, but it does indeed seem to be a detailed specification of Devil's vessel.
No offense, but it seems a little difficult to estimate your strength on the spot with this...
There isn't much reason for us to know every little detail about you, either.
We're just helping with the experiment anyway, and knowing too much might unduly influence the results.
As for the drill plan – I consider it more of a drill than an experiment... Ah, there it is. Likewise, it's totally unreadable.
The file's pretty big, sure, but this isn't an amount of data that should be exceeding your storage. Northampton and I didn't have a problem with it.
Huh, wonder what the problem is then. Maybe I downloaded too many of those movies Shangri-La recommended to me.
I mean, we're on Dr. Anzeel's network. Might as well use her bandwidth while we still can!
Look, I didn't even pirate them! Aren't you proud of me?
Excuse me, Commander? Can we strike that from the record? If Dr. Anzeel hears...
Y-Yorktown! Help me make sense of this data!
Y-your guess is as good as mine! I can't make heads or tails of it, ahaha...
Oh? Langley, would you mind explaining it to them?
*sigh*... Fine. First, the positions of the weapons on Devil's vessel.
Nnngh...
Grrrgh...
I can't understand a word Langley's saying...
Hammann, are you okay? There's steam coming out of your ears!
Ueergh... Bwuh?! What were we talking about?
This is where we were in the lesson. Do you have any questions so far?
"Langley, I think your teaching methods might not be well-suited to Hammann's learning style."
"We're not in a lecture hall right now, so try using a more appropriate method."
"For example, learning by demonstration."
"Devil, since you're on our side for now, we can have you help with this."
Want me to show off how my combat support drones work? Sure. I'll launch one now.
Whoa! My rigging is running so much smoother now! Is this because of Devil's drone?
It's not just smoother. I feel lighter, too... What in the world is that ability?
I just explained that. Devil uses her drones' discrete support modules to adjust the output of each rigging within range and augment their capabilities.
Each additional support increases these capabilities proportionately. As such...
Well, uh, I sure feel strong enough to do just about anything!
O-oh, nooo, Hamman. You can't go over theeere! I'd better go bring her back!
I wasn't done...
"Don't worry about it, Langley. You did a good job."
Well, thank you for the help... You're still a much better teacher than me, Commander.
D-don't be so down. I listened the whole time!
Anyway, it's about time we get started. Commander, shall we begin upon their return?
"Any more talk of data won't help much if they don't actually learn anything from it."
"I think everyone's ready for a proper demonstration. Let's experience her power for ourselves."

Testing Teamwork, Part 1.say: Everybody, the target ship is in position. It's right over there.
A small warship slowly comes into view from the distance.
Is that ship... some kind of hologram?
Nope. It's a mass-produced ship I released into this testing area.
You released it...? I didn't see anything on the radar.
Of course you didn't! It wasn't even here until I released it.
It wasn't here? Does that mean you brought it from somewhere else?
That's right. More precisely, I assembled it on the spot.
I keep the component parts in a manufacturing plant I manage, so I had its construction begin when we entered this area.
Once it was done, I transferred the ship here through my rigging, allowing me to deploy it in a place of my choosing.
It used to take around four days to make a mass-produced ship, but our technology allows me to shorten that to roughly forty minutes.
In other words, as long as you have enough materials on hand...
You can basically serve as a frontline shipyard and single-handedly provide a near-endless amount of reinforcements...
This kind of technology will forever change the way battles are fought...
Is that how the Sirens have been materializing out of nowhere...?
The way war is fought is always changing. There's nothing new about that.
Just as we shipgirls made traditional warships obsolete, the same may be true for the Antiochus.
Even though this technology is amazing, I don't think people will ever stop exploring new ideas.
Interesting. Really makes me wonder what the future has in store.
So, are you supposed to attack that target now?
Yes, but use live ammunition.
First, we'll establish a control to gauge your combat performance without the support of my drones.
I'll set up another target after that, and we will repeat the process except with my support.
Alrighty, let's do this!

Unfortunately, carriers will have to be left out of this part of the exercise. While carrier-based aircraft will benefit since they're also part of the rigging, this experiment specifically focuses on shelling performance.
Whoops! You're right. Good luck, the rest of you!

Testing Teamwork, Part 2.say: Target ship confirmed sunk. Shelling test complete.
The upgrades in raw output are quite nice, but the functions that highlight enemy weak points and analyze their current status are even more helpful.
My rigging even seems to have some sort of guided target correction built in.
Like aimbots in a shooting game?
I'm not much of a gamer, but that's probably a fairly good description.
Next up is the torpedo exercise. Carriers using torpedo bombers are able to participate, in addition to all torpedo-mounted ships.
What kind of assistance can your drones provide to torpedo bombers?
It can support single-target aiming, add a multi-target function through improved information processing capabilities, increase the precision of line-of-fire predictions...
...Change the travel path of torpedos after they've been launched, and even–
Basically, it draws out more performance than your weapons could on their own.
Unbelievable... How have you accomplished all this?
All I did was bring Dr. Aoste's research to life.
I have many other functionalities, which you are free to experiment with after we begin our exercises.
I can't wait to find out how strong I can get! Commander, just give us the signal when it's time to start!
Give me just a minute to deploy the mass-produced weapons you will be facing.

The Devil's Enforcers.say: With the Devil's support, we finish a series of bombing, recon, and electronic warfare tests.
Finally, it is time for us to face each other in a head-on test.
Man, I thought we'd be here all day, but those drones made things a piece of cake!
Despite your name, you're a real angel when it comes to buffing us up!
Lemme tell you, if I ever find myself on the battlefield, I'd want you behind my back!
Sure thing. I don't mind having meatshields like you.
I just hope... it never comes to that. War never accomplishes anything in the end.
Haha, relax! It's just a bit of friendly banter!
I understand you're just joking, but that is also our duty as shipgirls.
If war truly was to break out, I would proudly carry out that duty.
Yorktown's resolve seems to overlap with the memory of the Yorktown I know.
She sounds exactly like her. Even inside this simulation, I'm sure she must be the same person.
"Devil, are you ready to begin?"
Ready whenever you are.
Now, let's see how you fare in an actual battle against me.
Let me remind you that I'm not a frontline fighter. Supporting from the backlines is what I do best.
Having that said, my vessel is no slouch in a direct engagement.
In addition to my arsenal of support drones and defensive weapons, I obviously have the mobility to get out of dangerous situations.
Speaking of those drones, they will be used to strengthen the mass-produced ships to make your job harder.
While I do not hit as hard as my peers on my own, you will find it difficult to approach me.
My win condition is simple – I will isolate you, and pick you off one by one.
You oversee and coordinate the state of the battle then... Much like the Commander...
But the difference is, you are already isolated to begin with. Once we take out your mass-produced ships, there's nothing to protect you.
That's right! I bet I could take you out single-handedly!
Oh yeah? We'll see about that. Remember what I said about mass-produced weapons at the start?
Well... Just take a look around you.
What?! Yorktown, look at the radar... We're completely surrounded by unidentifiable signals!
Are these... also your drones? But, they look humanoid, just like your vessel...
They're technically both at once. Unlike me, however, they are not controlled by a mainframe.
How did you manage to produce that many vessels in such a short period of time?
I didn't, actually. My current vessel does not have the ability to mass-produce Enforcers, so I hid them beforehand.
As for why you didn't notice their presence... It's because I hacked into all your electronics before we even began.
When did you... Oh, I bet it was when you transferred all that data to us!
No wonder her communicator acted up... Here I thought she really had downloaded too many movies.
Hmph, make sure you take this lesson in electronic warfare to heart. Whoever controls information also controls the flow of battle.
Now, allow me to introduce my little helpers.
Enforcer XV: Temptation. Aircraft carrier. Specialty: using aircraft to harass targets and support allies.
Enforcer XV: Restriction. Battleship. Specialty: blowing targets to bits in head-on engagements.
This is the Antiochus force you'll be fighting. It's our integrated combat systems versus you and your Commander's wits.
Just so you know – this is probably not going to be a very fair fight.
Let's see... Enforcers, Enforcers... Found it. Commander, those Antiochus models boast absurd combat effectiveness.
They will make short work of us extremely quickly if we don't take this 100% seriously.
Against any other Arbiter, our meager forces would likely make any prospect of victory a pipe dream.
But I've fought plenty of Enforcers before. If direct combat is this Arbiter's weakness... then there might still be a chance.
Well said, Commander. Let's give it our all.
So, any final questions before we start?
I have one. Two, actually.
As long as it won't affect the experiment results, shoot.
Question one: your support drones can't buff us by mistake, can they?
Nope. They're calibrated to only empower Antiochus units.
Figures. Question two: are we using live ammunition or blanks?
Do you WANT to use live ammunition?
Oh, absolutely not. Dr. Aoste would cry if I kicked your asses AND left you full of holes.
You'll be the one crying at the end of this, KAN-SEN.
Big girls don't cry, and I'm bigger than you in more ways than one. I'll show you why I'm worthy of inheriting the title of Grey Ghost!
Hahaha! Those are fighting words! This will be interesting!

Thank You.say: Both our fleets arrive at the designated location. The exercise will begin any moment now.
While I look across the water from the deck of my command ship, Yorktown comes over to me.
Hi, Commander.
"I know that look. You have something to tell me."
Heehee. You read me like an open book.
I wanted to thank you again for all you've done for me.
For the longest time, sorrow formed a chain around my heart...
Then you came into my life. Spending time together with you released my heart from the clutches of grief after so long.
But a while ago, I started getting anxious. I feared that the wonderful moments we shared would come to an end.
You showed me the light. What would happen if I fell back into the darkness? My anxiety grew worse and worse.
You are still just as kind and gentle as you have always been, but...
Something about you feels more distant...
I can't exactly put my finger around it, but... It feels almost tragic.
I'm sorry, Commander. Please forget I brought this up...
It might just be my own doubts and insecurities speaking.
Thank you for always being so kind to me. Sometimes, I feel like that... is the true power behind the Type II rigging.
No... She's not wrong.
After all, I don't remember what kind of memories the "me" of this world might have shared with this Yorktown.
But despite that, this world has shown me how blissful Yorktown's smile can look.
And surely... there must be something real about that.
Hey, Yorktown~ Commander~
Hate to interrupt, just wanted to say you really should give your present to the Commander after this!
H-Hornet? How did you...?
Oh no... I forgot to switch off my communicator!
Wheeew~♪ You sure as heck did!
Don't tell me you heard everything I said...
Wheeew~♪ You sure as heck did!
Oh... my goodness...
Yorktown buries her face in her hands, blushing so furiously she seems to be giving off steam.
P-please ignore that! You know how she can be with her pranks sometimes...
Hornet, you should know better than to say stuff like that before an important exercise!
Yorktown... What's done is done...
Nobody else is here. Nobody else can see us. Nothing is going to change...
So, let's just say what we need to say...
Memphis...? Is something wrong?
Your voice is shaking... Did I say something I shouldn't have?
Sorry, I'm a bit worn out. I've just been going through a lot lately...
Once we're done here, I'm gonna get a good night's sleep. I'm sure everything will be fine when I wake up.
Yes, everything... will go back to normal...
There's no mistaking it. The tone of someone who has given in to despair. I've heard it before.
Without a doubt, she knows that this whole world is a temporary dream.
But, who exactly is she? She doesn't seem to be the same "Memphis" I know.
Ditto. After this is over, I'm taking more than a few days off!
I'm gonna go to the beach, binge-watch movies, and go full Bunker Hill mode and turn my sleep schedule on its head!
...Right. That's why we have to finish the work in front of us first.
Commander, radio Devil and tell her we're in position.
As my finger leaves the communicator, the battle begins.

The Arbiter's Transgression.say: Though our battle against Devil is gruelling to say the least, we are standing our ground.
Our carriers' planes weave past the anti-air fire and attack her repeatedly, but accomplish nothing other than create pillars of seawater.
How the hell is she so mobile when she's gotta haul that huge rigging around?!
What kind of power source did Dr. Aoste put into these second-generation Antiochus?!
It's as if she's using her sheer volume of Energy Cubes to brute force her way into the air.
Our torpedoes can't hit her and our bombers can't lock on to her. That leaves us with only fighters...
Yorktown, I still have these jets with experimental anti-air missiles. Do you want to give them a try?
If we can disable that pesky flight system, she'll be an easy target.
Commander, I'd like to request an aerial feint attack to give me an opportunity to coordinate with the missile fighter jets to strike Devil's power system.
Missiles aren't exactly foreign to me, but I've only seen them deployed in a very limited capacity by the Dragon Empery.
But here, the technology is robust enough to mount them onto carrier-based aircraft? Surely something that powerful should be able to disable her power system.
A direct hit! Commander, six of our missiles landed! That should be enough to...
Wait, it still didn't work...
She's still in the air! If anything, she's moving even faster now!
That doesn't make any sense. The module should've automatically shut down after the dummy warheads hit... Is something wrong with my sensors?
"Something isn't right here."
Commander, Enforcer aircraft incoming! I think those missiles pissed them off!
"Hammann, don't let them hit Yorktown."
On it! I'll shoot every last one of them down!
KABOOOM!
Ow! Oww! Those are some hard-hitting blanks...
Wait, no! Those are NOT blanks! Commander, Yorktown, they're using live ammunition!
Stupid Antiochus, breaking the rules once they start losing...
What?! Langley, are they using live rounds?
They are. I sent out planes to intercept theirs, and they went up in smoke. That couldn't happen if they shot blanks!
...I enlarge the feed from our recon planes and look carefully at Devil.
She no longer looks like the Antiochus vessel we had been speaking to just moments ago. The lust for destruction swirling around her is unmistakable - it is that of a Siren Arbiter.
Even as the shipgirls around me begin to realize the danger they are now in, they remain calm and collected.
Commander, what should we do now?
"Swap to live ammunition, and regroup around the command ship."
"Devil doesn't seem to be coming for us for the time being. She is an AI, so we can't exclude the possibility that she's gone rogue due to an error."
Understood! Everyone, group up!
The exercise can't go on like this. Should I call it off, Commander?
"You're right. I don't want to risk any damage if it can be avoided. Let's radio Devil."
"We'll need to determine why she suddenly used live ammo against us."
Roger.
Devil, you've violated the rules of the exercise! I order you to immediately disarm and return to the surface.
...She's not responding? Why isn't my override working?
What?! My hacking attempt failed! How? The Arbiters here can't have developed countermeasures already...
Only Anchorage and I were connected to the Reality Lens when the experiment started...
If "Memphis" was able to connect afterwards, it would be a piece of cake for the far more technologically advanced Arbiters.
Everything starts to fall into place... The Devil was not a product of this virtual space. Rather, this whole place was hosted by the Arbiter.
The fear of death sends an icy chill to my bones. This is no longer a situation any of us can manage.
"Change of plans. Turn around and head for Samos Island at full speed. Put me in touch with Anzeel and Aoste."
Roger!

Dissolution.say: [Reality Lens Replicated Space] [Location: Sea near Samos Island] [Cognitive merge: Commander - Arbiter: The Devil XV - ???]
I've downed another Enforcer!
How'd you like my Type II Edsall-class rigging, you big ol' tin can?
The Enforcers have much stronger defenses now than before. Be careful, everyone!
Engaging them rashly will only lead to unnecessary casualties!
Devil is beelining toward us! She'll be within sight in one minute!
Our bombers and torpedos failed to get any solid hits on the flying Arbiter, while our fighters couldn't break through the thick plating.
And, our missiles were all dummy warheads...
I can't reach Samos Island... The doctors aren't picking up, either!
"Are our communications being jammed?"
No, our comms systems are running fine. We simply can't reach them.
But it's okay, Commander! I'll think of another way!
Several vessels of unknown affiliation have appeared at close range! Where did they come from?!
"Enemy reinforcements now, of all times? Could they be Sirens?"
Our recon planes just sent an image! Wait a second...
What the...? I can't make anything out!
The image Northampton sent us was full of... them.
Commander, do you know what these enemies are?
Though the images should've been processed, my eyes are unable to focus on the blurry mass.
Their forms seem to shift within the still images. I see images of a nightmarish, writhing mass engulfing the battlefield.
For some reason, there's a gnawing emptiness in the back of my mind. Where have I seen these enemies before?
The City of Light in the Arcana Sanctum flashes through my mind.
But what happened next? How did we deal with these enemies?
KABOOOM!
Oof! These enemies don't mess around!
Yeah, not to mention they're covered in some sort of... weird mist...
They look like Sirens, but there's something very different about these things!
If they look like Sirens and come attacking us, then as far as I'm concerned, they might as well be Sirens!
Well said! Either way, there's but one thing for us to do: dispatch them!
That's the Kirov I know! Hahaha!
Right you are! I cannot and will not rest until the revolution has come!
They are neither immune to our cannon fire, nor too fast to hit! We need only keep firing on them until they sink!
Blow them to dust, comrades! Hold the frontline until Comrade Commander and Gremyashchy have evacuated!
The City of Light, the memories of Anzeel and Aoste, the Long Island Defense Line, Recorder, the end of the battle with this unknown enemy...
Memories start to flood back into my head.
"Please fight until the last light has faded."
...Why? Why have these things appeared here?
Those should have been excluded from the components of this world!
Even if everyone inside this space has awoken, there still shouldn't be any way for them to get inside...!
Does Devil somehow have memories of that battle?!
Or does the Tower? Or the Natural Calculation System itself?
How... What have I done?!
Who could have awakened these memories?!
Memphis, what's wrong?! Get a hold of yourself!
The memories of that battle... They belong to me...
It's my fault for observing the Commander and Anchorage!
It's my fault for making the Commander flee here!
It's my fault for connecting to the Natural Calculation System!
The Tower already noticed that these memories of ruination were here!
...And that's why I can't control the Reality Lens or access the Natural Calculation System anymore!
There's no escaping this place now!
We can't free the Commander's consciousness!
And if this world is destroyed, the Commander's consciousness will die with it!
I'm sorry, I'm sorry! It's all my fault... I've...
I've doomed... the Commander... *sob*...
What have I done?!
I silently pull Memphis into a hug.
Memphis, what are you going on about?!
Nothing you're saying makes any sense! What's going to happen to the Commander?!
I'm sorry... *sob*... I'm sorry!
Commander... I...
Memphis, Commander! It's not over yet!
Who's there...? And where are you?
That voice... Helena?!
Reality Lens connection confirmed. Removing data lock.
This burden isn't yours alone to bear, Memphis.
Is that really you, Helena? You look so different...
*sniffle*... Helena...
Who... are you two?
Explaining would take too long, so maybe another... No. I'll try to make it as brief as I can now.
I'm listening.
I am Helena, but I am not the Helena you know.
Likewise, she is Memphis, but she is not the Memphis you know.
Like you all, we are loyal to the Commander.
Sorry to keep this introduction so brief, but we're going to do everything in our power to save the Commander.
Is that a satisfactory explanation, Commander?
Commander, can we trust them?
"That's an easy one."
Okay. If you say so.
Long time no see, Commander.
"I could tell that Memphis didn't belong to this virtual space."
"But I didn't expect you to be involved as well."
"Then my memories of the Sea of Stars being attacked..."
Mhm. Things went awry when you used the Reality Lens to connect to Anchorage's hull.
Instead, you got connected to the Natural Calculation System – the origin of the Reality Lens – by mistake. That's where you saw those memories of Dr. Anzeel.
Unfortunately, the Arbiter caught wind of this and came after you.
When Dr. Anzeel's failsafe detected this, it erased the entire virtual space, flinging your consciousness into limbo.
Wait. Something's coming this way.
Whatever it is, it's been following you for a while now.
Warning: Category V threat detected nearby. Commencing removal of AI and records per security protocol.
Teacher!
Hurry... We need to run!
Teacher! This is bad...! Nooo!
"So, everything began when I saw that aberration within Anchorage's hull data. That's when I got pulled into Memphis's virtual space where the Sea of Stars came under attack."
"Even so, the Arbiter continued to chase me, and she had no choice but to link multiple hulls together to create this megaspace."
"This all culminated in the creation of this entire world from several different memories and minds. Meanwhile, I never woke up at all."
That's why Memphis blamed herself for what happened here. This place is a simulacrum of all the consciousnesses connected to it.
Yours, hers, the Devil's, and even the records buried at the foundation of the Natural Calculation System.
The reason she hosted the virtual space from the Devil's mainframe was to prevent your mind from being contaminated by the aberrations you just saw.

Incidentally, when your consciousness melded with the existing data in the Natural Calculation System, your compassion for your shipgirls provided the last missing piece of data needed for the completion of the Type II rigging.
Memphis superimposed your memories with the existing memories of the Type II rigging, creating a world where these shipgirls would temporarily be freed from their fates.
Our only miscalculation was, the Devil found some way to forcibly connect into this space at the same time.
That was why her vessel went berserk and started attacking us with live ammunition.
And that brings us to the present moment.
"I'm still extremely confused, but I get the gist of your explanation."
"But there's one question you haven't answered. What does the Tower have to do with all of this?"
Sometimes, Commander, ignorance is bliss.
"Huh?"
Don't worry about it. What's important is that the situation isn't as bad as Memphis thinks.
The other Helena and I can regain control of this virtual space and extract you from the Reality Lens. To do that though, you'll have to boot out the other intruder.
You know what to do, right? You said it yourself – "Please fight until..."
"...The last light has faded. And fight I will until everyone's safe."
Of course, Commander!
I've got your back. Yours, too, Memphis.
...Thank you!

To Topple a Devil.say: [Reality Lens Replicated Space] [Cognitive merge: Commander - The Devil XV - Framework of Logic - ???]
The Devil's formation is already in place. Under these conditions, don't even bother trying to attack the main vessel.
We'll need to eliminate her "eyes" first – the Temptation-type carriers.
Memphis. Requesting intel support.
